text: The sleekness of glass, the sterile orderliness of this space, all sharply angled and illuminated, makes me feel alert but unsettled. The environment is engineered for clarity and control, yet I sense a coldness, a detachment from the organic pulse of life. The R+D wall, bold and vibrant, signals a quest for progress—an ode to human ingenuity and our collaboration with machines. In this boundary between artificial structure and imaginative future, I find myself questioning the true nature of advancement: does it serve to connect us more deeply, or to isolate us behind reflective surfaces?
